entries.oneLiner: Directive at the top of a Next.js or React file that marks the module and its imports as Client Components, opting them out of server-only execution.
Directive that marks a function (or all functions in a module) as a Server Action — callable from client code but executed on the server.
Next.js route segment config that forces every request to render dynamically on the server, opting out of static generation and caching.
Next.js route segment config that forces a route to be statically generated at build time and never re-render on demand.
Next.js route segment config (number of seconds) that controls how often a statically generated route is regenerated in the background.
React hook that runs a side effect after a component renders. Use it for subscriptions, manual DOM mutations, and synchronising with external systems — not for data computation.

docLinks.title: You Might Not Need an Effect

entries.oneLiner: React hook that adds reactive local state to a function component. Returns the current value and a setter that triggers a re-render when called.
React hook that memoises an expensive computation between renders. Only recomputes when its dependency list changes.
React hook that returns a memoised function reference that only changes when its dependencies change. Useful for stable callbacks passed to memoised children.
React boundary that lets you declaratively show a fallback UI while its children are loading data, code, or other async work.
A React component that renders only on the server. It can access server-only resources (DB, filesystem, secrets) and ships zero JavaScript to the client.
A React component that runs in the browser (and pre-renders on the server). Marked with the 'use client' directive at the top of the file. Required for state, effects, and browser-only APIs.
The process where React attaches event handlers and state to server-rendered HTML in the browser, turning static markup into an interactive app.
Code that runs before a request completes — rewrites, redirects, headers, auth checks. In Next.js, lives in middleware.ts at the project root and runs at the edge.
Next.js function exported from a dynamic route segment to specify which paths should be statically generated at build time.
